description: Unity 6 View layer for Favor of the Pharaoh. Implements IPlayerInput via async UI interactions. Reuses Tom's original art assets from Assets/. Top-down board camera. Scene-per-screen. Deterministic dice (engine rolls, Unity animates). Unity 6.3 at C:\Program Files\Unity\Hub\Editor\6000.3.6f1. References FotP.Engine.dll built from src/FotP.Engine/.

nodes.title: Create Unity 6 project and import engine DLL
nodes.description: Create a new Unity 6 project under the repo (e.g. unity/ directory). Use URP (Universal Render Pipeline) for modern rendering with 2D/3D flexibility. Import FotP.Engine.dll as a managed plugin. Verify the engine DLL loads and GameState can be instantiated from a MonoBehaviour. Copy Tom's art assets from the old Assets/Textures/ and Assets/Materials/ into the new project. Set up 3 scenes: MainMenu, Game, Results. Unity path: C:\Program Files\Unity\Hub\Editor\6000.3.6f1\Editor. NOTE: Claude cannot run Unity Editor directly. Create the project structure, scripts, and scene configs as files. The user will open in Unity to verify.

nodes.title: GameController MonoBehaviour
nodes.description: Central MonoBehaviour that owns GameState and GameRunner. Lives in the Game scene. On scene load: creates GameState, calls Setup() with player configs, creates GameRunner. Runs the game loop via async/coroutine â€” GameRunner.RunGame() is synchronous so wrap each turn step in a coroutine yield or use UniTask for async. Exposes events that Views subscribe to: OnTurnStarted, OnDiceRolled, OnDiceLocked, OnTileClaimed, OnPhaseChanged, OnGameOver. Holds reference to UnityPlayerInput. File: unity/Assets/Scripts/GameController.cs.

nodes.title: UnityPlayerInput async IPlayerInput implementation
nodes.description: Implements IPlayerInput where each method blocks (via async/Task or coroutine) until the player makes a UI choice. Pattern for each method: 1. Show relevant UI (highlight clickable dice, show buttons, etc). 2. Set a TaskCompletionSource<T> or callback. 3. Await player click/selection. 4. Hide UI, return the selected value. Methods to implement (all async): - ChooseDiceToLock: highlight active dice, player clicks to toggle, Done button - ChooseContinueRolling: show Roll Again / Stop buttons - ChooseTileToClaim: highlight claimable market tiles, click or Pass - ChooseDie: highlight candidate dice, click one - ChooseMultipleDice: highlight candidates, toggle select, Done - ChoosePipValue: number picker (1-6) popup - ChooseScarab: show scarab panel, click one or Pass - ChoosePlayer: show player portraits, click one - ChooseYesNo: Yes/No dialog - ChooseUseAbility: tile glow + click or Skip File: unity/Assets/Scripts/UI/UnityPlayerInput.cs. This is the most critical piece â€” every player interaction flows through it.

nodes.title: BoardView - market bars and tile display
nodes.description: Top-down board layout showing: - 5 level bars (levels 3-7) arranged vertically - Each bar has slots with tile stacks (use Tom's tile textures) - Tile stacks show top tile face, count indicator for remaining - Empty slots grayed out - Claimable tiles highlight when in Claim phase Subscribe to GameController events to update when tiles are claimed. Use Tom's textures from Assets/Textures/ for tile art. File: unity/Assets/Scripts/Views/BoardView.cs.

nodes.title: DiceView - cup, active area, locked pyramid
nodes.description:
Three zones rendered on the board: - Cup area: shows dice count, click to roll - Active area: rolled dice displayed with face values, clickable for
  locking and ability targeting
- Locked area: pyramid arrangement of locked dice Dice rolling animation: when engine rolls, animate the die spinning and landing on the determined value. Can be simple 2D flip animation or 3D tumble â€” start simple (2D sprite swap with a brief shake). Use Tom's dice textures from Assets/Dice/ and Assets/Textures/. Subscribe to zone SmartList events (OnAdded/OnRemoved) to move dice visuals between areas. File: unity/Assets/Scripts/Views/DiceView.cs.

nodes.title: Player UI panels
nodes.description: UI overlay showing: - Current player indicator and turn phase - Owned tiles bar (scrollable, shows tile abilities) - Scarab token display (reroll/pip/die counts) - Status text for prompts ("Choose a die to lock", etc) - Done/Cancel buttons for multi-step selections - Score display (pyramid total) Use Unity UI Toolkit or legacy Canvas â€” Canvas is simpler for a first pass. Player tiles use Tom's artwork. File: unity/Assets/Scripts/Views/PlayerUI.cs.

nodes.title: Main Menu scene
nodes.description: Simple menu scene: - Game title (Favor of the Pharaoh) - Player count selection (2-4) - Player name entry (with AI toggle per player) - Bar side selection (A or B) - Random tiles toggle - Play button â†’ loads Game scene with config Use ScriptableObject or static config class to pass settings between scenes. File: unity/Assets/Scripts/UI/MainMenuController.cs.

nodes.title: Results scene
nodes.description: End-of-game display: - Winner announcement with player name - Final scores (pyramid totals) - Tiles collected by each player - Rematch button (reload Game scene) - Main Menu button File: unity/Assets/Scripts/UI/ResultsController.cs.

nodes.title: Integration test - play a full game in Unity
nodes.description: Wire everything together and verify a complete game can be played: 1. Start from Main Menu, configure 2 players (1 human, 1 AI) 2. Play through several turns: roll dice, lock, claim tiles 3. Verify scarab spending works 4. Verify tile abilities trigger and prompt correctly 5. Play to roll-off and verify winner is displayed This is a manual test â€” document the steps for the user to verify in the Unity Editor play mode. File: unity/Assets/Scripts/Tests/IntegrationTestChecklist.md.
depends_on: BoardView - market bars and tile display
DiceView - cup, active area, locked pyramid
